summary:
Older patients who refuse self-management interventions in the Netherlands do not differ from those who agree in terms of baseline health status or functional ability. Instead, refusal is more closely tied to concerns about using technology than to poorer physical or mental health conditions. 

Final Answer: B

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant differences related to patient characteristics such as health status and functionality.
